question: Who hosted the 97th Academy Awards ceremony?
source_sentence: Comedian Conan O'Brien hosted the show for the first time.

question: How many awards did Anora win at the 97th Academy Awards?
source_sentence: Anora won a leading five awards, including Best Picture.

question: How many viewers in the United States watched the 97th Academy Awards telecast?
source_sentence: The telecast drew 19.69 million viewers in the United States.

question: Who became the first member of Generation Z to win an acting Oscar at the 97th Academy Awards?
source_sentence: At the age of 25, Best Actress winner Mikey Madison became the first member of Generation Z to win an acting Oscar.

question: How long was Adrien Brody's acceptance speech at the 97th Oscars, setting the record for longest in Oscar history?
source_sentence: Best Actor winner Adrien Brody gave the longest acceptance speech in Oscar history at 5 minutes and 37 seconds.

question: Who became the first Dominican American to win an Oscar at the 97th Academy Awards?
source_sentence: Best Supporting Actress winner Zoe Saldaña was the first Dominican American to win an Oscar.

question: Who became the first Black man to win the Oscar for Best Costume Design?
source_sentence: Best Costume Design winner Paul Tazewell became the first Black man to win that category.

question: Who presented the 'In Memoriam' segment at the 97th Academy Awards?
source_sentence: The annual "In Memoriam" segment, presented by Morgan Freeman, featured the Los Angeles Master Chorale performing "Lacrimosa" from Requiem by Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart.

question: Who received the Jean Hersholt Humanitarian Award at the 97th Academy Awards' Governors Awards?
source_sentence: Richard Curtis – "A brilliant comedic storyteller [with] tremendous charitable efforts".

question: How many nominations did Emilia Pérez receive at the 97th Oscars, the most for a non-English-language film?
source_sentence: Emilia Pérez led all nominees with thirteen nominations, the most for a non-English-language film in Oscars history.

question: How many nominations did the film Sinners receive at the 98th Academy Awards, setting a new Oscar record?
source_sentence: The sixteen nominations received by Sinners are the most in Oscar history, surpassing the previous record of fourteen shared by All About Eve (1950), Titanic (1997), and La La Land (2016).

question: What new category was introduced at the 98th Academy Awards?
source_sentence: This year, Best Casting will be presented as a new category, bringing the total number of competitive Oscar categories to 24.

question: Who received the Jean Hersholt Humanitarian Award at the 98th Academy Awards' Governors Awards?
source_sentence: Dolly Parton – A "beloved performer [who] exemplifies the spirit of the Jean Hersholt Humanitarian Award through her unwavering dedication to charitable efforts".

question: Who announced the nominees for the 98th Academy Awards?
source_sentence: The nominees for the 98th Academy Awards were announced on January 22, 2026, at the Samuel Goldwyn Theater in Beverly Hills, by actress Danielle Brooks and actor Lewis Pullman.

question: Who became the most-nominated Black woman across any Oscar category at the 98th ceremony?
source_sentence: Ruth E. Carter became the most-nominated Black woman, across any category, with her fifth nomination for Best Costume Design.

question: Who became the first woman of color nominated for Best Cinematography at the 98th Oscars?
source_sentence: Autumn Durald Arkapaw became the fourth woman and the first woman of color nominated for Best Cinematography.

question: Who became the most-nominated female producer at the 98th Academy Awards?
source_sentence: With nine nominations for Best Picture, Dede Gardner became the most-nominated female producer, breaking her tie with Kathleen Kennedy.

question: Who became the first Brazilian nominated for Best Actor in a Leading Role at the 98th Oscars?
source_sentence: Wagner Moura became the first Brazilian nominated for Best Actor in a Leading Role.

question: Which actress set the record for the longest gap between Oscar nominations at the 98th Academy Awards?
source_sentence: The 40-year span between Amy Madigan's first nomination, for her supporting role in Twice in a Lifetime (1985), and her second, for Weapons, set the record for the longest gap between Oscar nominations for an actress.

question: Which nominated casting director said 'people really don't understand what casting directors do' during a panel about the new Best Casting category?
source_sentence: "I think it's kind of sad that it took that long," said Cassandra Kulukundis, nominated for One Battle After Another, adding, "I'm realizing on this journey that people really don't understand what [casting directors] do."

question: Who hosted the 82nd Golden Globe Awards, becoming the first solo female host in Golden Globes history?
source_sentence: The event was hosted by comedian Nikki Glaser, making her the first solo female host in Golden Globes history.

question: Who received the Cecil B. DeMille Award at the 82nd Golden Globes?
source_sentence: The recipient of the Cecil B. DeMille Award was Viola Davis, while the Carol Burnett Award was awarded to Ted Danson.

question: Who received the Carol Burnett Award at the 82nd Golden Globe Awards?
source_sentence: The recipient of the Cecil B. DeMille Award was Viola Davis, while the Carol Burnett Award was awarded to Ted Danson.

question: Which firm took over tabulating the nominations at the 82nd Golden Globes from Ernst & Young?
source_sentence: This year's nominations were tabulated by KPMG, the U.S. audit, tax, and advisory firm, taking over Ernst & Young after fifty plus years.

question: Which distributor received the most total nominations between film and television at the 82nd Golden Globes?
source_sentence: Netflix received a total of 36 nominations between film and television, the most of any distributor.

question: How many total nominations did Netflix receive at the 82nd Golden Globe Awards?
source_sentence: Netflix received a total of 36 nominations between film and television, the most of any distributor.

question: Who became the first Brazilian actress to win a Golden Globe in an acting category at the 82nd Golden Globes?
source_sentence: Fernanda Torres became the first Brazilian actress to win a Golden Globe in an acting category, as well as the first Brazilian, first South American and first Portuguese-speaking actor to win the Golden Globe Award for Best Actress in a Motion Picture – Drama.

question: How many nominations did Emilia Pérez receive at the 82nd Golden Globe Awards?
source_sentence: Emilia Pérez, with 10 nominations, became the most nominated comedy/musical film in Golden Globes history, just 1 shy of the overall record of 11, set by Robert Altman's country music satire Nashville in the drama category in 1976.

question: Which two presenters were originally announced for the 82nd Golden Globes but were not included in the final running order?
source_sentence: Furthermore, Aubrey Plaza and Rob McElhenney were originally announced as presenters on January 2, 2025, but were not included in the final running order on the day of the ceremony.

question: Which television series led the TV nominations at the 82nd Golden Globe Awards with 5 nominations?
source_sentence: Additionally, The Bear led the television nominations with 5, followed by Only Murders in the Building and Shōgun with 4 each.

question: Who hosted the 81st Golden Globe Awards ceremony?
source_sentence: Comedian Jo Koy hosted the ceremony.

question: Which two new categories debuted at the 81st Golden Globe Awards?
answer: Cinematic and Box Office Achievement and Best Performance in Stand-Up Comedy on Television
source_sentence: The ceremony also featured the debut of two new categories: "Cinematic and Box Office Achievement" and "Best Performance in Stand-Up Comedy on Television"

question: Who became the first Indigenous actor to win a Golden Globe Award at the 81st ceremony?
source_sentence: For her portrayal of Mollie Burkhart in Killers of the Flower Moon, Lily Gladstone became the first Indigenous actor to win a Golden Globe Award, winning for Best Actress in a Motion Picture – Drama.

question: Which network acquired the broadcast rights for the 81st Golden Globe Awards, replacing NBC?
source_sentence: On November 17, 2023, it was announced that CBS had acquired the broadcast rights to the ceremony, replacing the Golden Globes' long-time U.S. broadcast partner NBC.

question: How many combined nominations did Barbenheimer receive at the 81st Golden Globes?
source_sentence: Additionally, the cultural phenomenon of "Barbenheimer" received seventeen nominations, winning seven.

question: How many voters threatened to withhold their final-round votes in protest at the 81st Golden Globe Awards?
source_sentence: That same month, TheWrap reported that 64 voters threatened to withhold their final-round votes in protest, after being told that they would not be given tickets to the ceremony.

question: How many journalists made up the new group of voters selecting the nominees and winners at the 81st Golden Globes?
source_sentence: With the awards no longer being overseen by the HFPA, a new, racially and ethnically diverse group of voters consisting of 300 journalists representing 76 countries selected the 2023 nominees and winners.

question: How many countries were represented by the new group of voters at the 81st Golden Globe Awards?
source_sentence: With the awards no longer being overseen by the HFPA, a new, racially and ethnically diverse group of voters consisting of 300 journalists representing 76 countries selected the 2023 nominees and winners.

question: Which two companies acquired the Golden Globes' rights and properties from the HFPA?
answer: Dick Clark Productions and Eldridge Industries
source_sentence: On June 12, 2023, the HFPA announced that all of its rights and properties related to the Golden Globes were acquired by Dick Clark Productions and Eldridge Industries.

question: Who co-hosted the announcement of the 81st Golden Globes nominations?
answer: Cedric the Entertainer and Wilmer Valderrama
source_sentence: The nominations were announced on December 11, 2023, during an event co-hosted by Cedric the Entertainer and Wilmer Valderrama.

question: Who hosted the 77th BAFTA Film Awards ceremony?
source_sentence: The ceremony was hosted by David Tennant for the first time.

question: Who received the BAFTA Fellowship at the 77th British Academy Film Awards?
source_sentence: Samantha Morton

question: Who received the Outstanding British Contribution to Cinema award at the 77th BAFTAs?
source_sentence: June Givanni

question: How many wins did Oppenheimer earn at the 77th BAFTAs, the most of any film?
source_sentence: The epic biographical thriller film Oppenheimer received the most nominations with thirteen and the most wins with seven, including Best Film, Best Director (Christopher Nolan) and Best Actor (Cillian Murphy).

question: Who performed 'Murder on the Dancefloor' at the 77th BAFTA Film Awards ceremony?
source_sentence: English singer and songwriter Sophie Ellis-Bextor performed her 2001 chart-topper "Murder on the Dancefloor", most recently known as the concluding viral song from the five-time BAFTA-nominated film Saltburn.

question: Who delivered the musical performance of 'Time After Time' during the In Memoriam segment at the 77th BAFTAs?
source_sentence: Additionally, British actress and television presenter Hannah Waddingham delivered an exclusive musical performance of the Cyndi Lauper song "Time After Time" during the "In Memoriam" segment.

question: Who gatecrashed the Best Film acceptance speech at the 77th BAFTAs?
source_sentence: While producer Emma Thomas was accepting the award for Best Film on behalf of Oppenheimer, social media personality Lizwani gatecrashed her speech.

question: Who announced the nominees for the 77th British Academy Film Awards?
source_sentence: The nominations were announced via livestream by former EE Rising Star Award nominees Naomi Ackie and Kingsley Ben-Adir, from the arts charity's HQ at 195 Piccadilly, London, on 18 January 2024.

question: How many nominations did Poor Things receive at the 77th BAFTAs?
source_sentence: Ultimately, Oppenheimer received the most nominations with thirteen, followed by Poor Things with eleven.

question: Who hosted the red carpet exclusively on BAFTA's TikTok channel at the 77th BAFTAs?
source_sentence: while Zainab Jiwa was live from the red carpet exclusively on BAFTA's TikTok channel from 15:00–16:00 GMT.

question: Who received the BAFTA Fellowship at the 78th British Academy Film Awards?
source_sentence: Warwick Davis

question: Which organisation received the Outstanding British Contribution to Cinema award at the 78th BAFTAs?
source_sentence: MediCinema

question: How many personal nominations did Rich Peppiatt receive at the 78th BAFTAs, making him the most-nominated debut director?
source_sentence: filmmaker Rich Peppiatt became the most-nominated debut director at the BAFTAs with four personal nominations for Kneecap.

question: Which film did Rich Peppiatt direct that earned him four personal BAFTA nominations at the 78th ceremony?
source_sentence: filmmaker Rich Peppiatt became the most-nominated debut director at the BAFTAs with four personal nominations for Kneecap.

question: Who won the EE Rising Star Award at the 78th BAFTAs, considered a surprising result?
source_sentence: Winners considered surprising were David Jonsson for the EE Rising Star Award and Mikey Madison winning Best Actress in a Leading Role.

question: Which song did David Tennant perform in his introduction to the 78th BAFTA ceremony?
source_sentence: In his introduction to the ceremony, Tennant performed "I'm Gonna Be (500 Miles)" by The Proclaimers before his monologue full of dad jokes.

question: Who performed 'As Time Goes By' on piano during the In Memoriam segment at the 78th BAFTAs?
source_sentence: The In Memoriam was accompanied by actor Jeff Goldblum, performing "As Time Goes By" on piano, and paid tribute to the following.

question: Which new category was awarded for the first time at the 78th BAFTA Film Awards?
source_sentence: The Best Children's & Family Film was awarded for the first time in 2025, having been announced in 2023 as the first new category since the introduction of Best Casting in 2020.

question: Who announced the nominations for the 78th BAFTA Film Awards?
source_sentence: The nominations for all the other categories were unveiled on 15 January 2025 by previous BAFTA Award winners Mia McKenna-Bruce and Will Sharpe.

question: Which Take That song was performed at the 78th BAFTA ceremony, having featured prominently in the nominee Anora?
source_sentence: Ahead of the acting and other major awards being announced, Take That performed their song "Greatest Day", which featured prominently in nominee Anora.
